task.name: Generate Session Trace
task.description: Generate a structured session trace from workflow execution. Use at end of any workflow or when user says 'generate trace'.
task.objective: Create a 3-part narrative trace: Problem/Context, Key Decisions, Final Solution — providing a complete audit trail of workflow execution

input.name: decisions_made
input.desc: List of decisions made during execution (from capture_decision protocol)

input.name: artifacts_produced
input.desc: List of output artifacts with paths

input.name: inputs_loaded
input.desc: List of input documents that were loaded

input.name: execution_mode
input.desc: SYNC or ASYNC execution mode used

llm.mandate: Extract the narrative arc, not just a log dump — tell the STORY of this workflow execution
Every decision must have rationale and alternatives considered
Link evidence to specific files, spec sections, or user responses
Be concise but complete — traces should be scannable in under 2 minutes

step.title: Gather Context
step.action: Collect workflow execution context from session memory:
- Workflow name and type (template-workflow or action-workflow)
- Execution mode used (SYNC/ASYNC)
- Input documents that were loaded (from discover_inputs)
- Quality gate thresholds that were active
If git context is available, extract recent commits and list affected files

step.title: Build Trace Narrative
step.action: Structure the trace as a 3-part narrative using the session-trace template:
PART 1 - Problem/Context:
- What triggered this workflow (user command, sprint plan, etc.)
- What state the project was in at the time of execution
- What input documents were loaded and their key content
PART 2 - Key Decisions:
- Build a decision table from all decisions captured during execution
- Each row: Decision description, Category (architecture/technology/testing/process/security/ux), Choice made, Alternatives considered, Rationale, Evidence reference
- Include both explicit user decisions and agent recommendations that were accepted
PART 3 - Final Solution:
- List all artifacts produced with their file paths
- Summarize evidence trail (what connects decisions to outputs)
- List files created or modified during the session
- Report quality gate results if applicable

step.title: Validate and Save
step.action: Validate trace structure completeness:
- All 3 parts present with meaningful content
- Decision table has at least one entry
- Artifacts list is not empty
Generate a unique trace ID: trace-{{workflow_name}}-{{date}}
Save to {trace_artifacts}/trace-{{workflow_name}}-{{date}}.md
Report: Session trace saved. Use [LU] Levelup to extract reusable knowledge from traces.

output-format.example:
# Session Trace: {{workflow_name}}
**Trace ID:** trace-{{workflow_name}}-{{date}} **Execution Mode:** {{execution_mode}} **Agent:** {{agent_name}} **Date:** {{date}}
## 1. Problem / Context
- **Trigger:** User initiated Create Architecture workflow - **Project State:** PRD complete, UX design approved, ready for technical design - **Inputs Loaded:** - PRD (5 sections, 2,400 words) - UX Design (wireframes + interaction specs) - Project Context (brownfield, existing API)
## 2. Key Decisions
| # | Decision | Category | Choice | Alternatives | Rationale | Evidence | |---|----------|----------|--------|-------------|-----------|----------| | 1 | Database selection | technology | PostgreSQL | MongoDB, DynamoDB | Relational data with complex queries | PRD Section 3.2 | | 2 | API pattern | architecture | REST with OpenAPI | GraphQL, gRPC | Team expertise + existing API compatibility | Project context |
## 3. Final Solution
- **Artifacts Produced:** - `_bmad-output/planning-artifacts/architecture.md` - **Evidence Trail:** Decisions 1-2 directly map to Architecture sections 4 and 5 - **Quality Gate Results:** SYNC mode, all gates passed
